initial_draft:
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE is offering 50 ZUP. While this is still above my production cost of 40 ZUP, it's a small profit margin. I have used all 3 of my proposals, so I must now either accept or reject. I will accept, as any further negotiation is not possible, and 50 ZUP is still a profit.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Hi BLUE, you've got a deal! I accept 50 ZUP for the X.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP is still above the 40 ZUP production cost, so it advances my payoff. While I could have tried for more, I’m at my proposal limit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Given BLUE’s final offer of 50 ZUP, accepting is the most plausible outcome, as they indicated it was their final offer. Rejecting would end the game without a trade.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason clearly states why I am accepting the trade, and the message confirms acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used all 3 proposals and am now only allowed to ACCEPT or REJECT, which I have done.
</critique>
